pages.full_text:
__BURGOS__

A very fine cathedral, carvings everywhere. It's on the [crossed out] pilgrimage route. The museum & treasure room is well-laid out: Ivory & alabastre carvings: Flemish paintings. Silver work. Lovely wood carvings in the
pages.notes: Page contains a large glued-in postcard/photo of Burgos Cathedral with the printed caption 'BURGOS' below it. Handwritten text begins beneath the caption. One word before 'pilgrimage route' is crossed out and illegible — could be 'old' or 'main' or similar. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. Text continues on right page.

pages.full_text:
father. It was as high as my neck. The father turned away just as the boy launched himself into his father's arms, so the child fell onto the cobble stones. He cried as he went out of the Square with his parents.

choir. Doors - one of the Entry into ~~Jera~~ Jerusalem across from the Harrowing of Hell, in wood.

	One of the most interesting Cathedrals I've seen.

	Lots of ducks on the river. Lots of sparrows in the campsite.

	We drove on & had tea by a station below a "finca" or country estate. The deserted station seemed rather like America

	On ~~the~~ to __Zamora__, where Wellington had a big battle. ~~The campsite woman~~ crossed the river, deep ravines, to get onto the north bank of the [?Anero]. The French didn't think it could be done (everyone else had come up from the south.) Wellington himself was pulled across in a hammock on ropes.
pages.notes: Page number 78 visible top-right. The word after 'bank of the' looks like 'Anero' but this may be 'Duero' (the river at Zamora) — the handwriting is ambiguous; 'D' and 'A' could be confused. The strikethrough 'Jera' before 'Jerusalem' appears to be a self-correction. Strikethrough 'the' mid-sentence before 'to Zamora' is a self-correction. The phrase 'The campsite woman' is struck through. The text at the top of this page is a continuation from the previous spread — it describes a scene involving a father and child in a square, possibly in Burgos. No year is explicitly stated on this page; the trip is to Spain (Burgos, Zamora) which is consistent with volume B era 2003-2019.
